data.title: Farmers & Merchants Bancorp posts Q1 2026 update: $5.8B assets, 15.71% RBC, 4.25% NIM
data.description:
Farmers & Merchants Bancorp released a Q1 2026 investor presentation highlighting $5.8 billion in assets, a preliminary 15.71% RBC and a 4.25% NIM.

Key Highlights:

  • Investor presentation furnished under Regulation FD with updates on financial position, business and operations.
  • Balance sheet: $5.8B assets; preliminary risk-based capital 15.71%; tax-equivalent NIM 4.25% as of 3/31/26.
  • Strong liquidity: $1.6B investment portfolio (TE yield 3.70%), no other borrowings or brokered CDs as of 3/31/26.
  • Loan/deposit mix: 58% fixed / 42% variable loans; loan-to-deposit ratio 71.04%; cost of avg total deposits 1.18%.
  • Credit quality: ACL $76.9M (2.12% of loans); one non-performing CRE loan $730k; net recoveries $43k in Q1-26.

Original SEC Filing: FARMERS & MERCHANTS BANCORP [ FMCB ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Coeur Mining Completes Private Exchange, Issues $385.8 Million 6.875% Senior Notes Due 2032
data.description:
Coeur Mining completed a private exchange offer and issued $385.8 million of 6.875% Senior Notes due 2032 under a new indenture. The notes were exchanged for existing $400 million New Gold 6.875% notes, with no cash proceeds to Coeur apart from fees and cash consideration tied to the exchange. The unsecured notes, guaranteed by certain subsidiaries, pay interest semi-annually and include standard redemption and change-of-control protections. The transaction refinances obligations and enhances Coeur's capital structure flexibility.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Indenture governing 6.875% Senior Notes due 2032 (private exchange)
  • Counterparty: Bank of New York Mellon, as trustee; Eligible holders of New Gold 6.875% notes
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 22 2026 / Apr 22 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Matures 2032
  • Reason: Refinance obligations and improve capital structure flexibility

Original SEC Filing: Coeur Mining, Inc. [ CDE ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Caliber Advances Hyatt Studios Platform; Steamboat Springs Breaks Ground After Closing Construction Financing
data.description:
Caliber advances a three-market Hyatt Studios development platform and closed acquisition and construction financing for the Steamboat Springs project, which is expected to break ground in Q2 2026.

Key Highlights:

  • Closed acquisition and construction financing for Steamboat Springs; construction expected to begin Q2 2026.
  • Three Hyatt Studios projects advancing in Steamboat Springs, Riverwalk/Scottsdale, and Georgetown to target supply-constrained markets.
  • Steamboat targeted to open Q3–Q4 2027; typical development timeline ~15–17 months from groundbreaking to opening.
  • Projects designed to transition into Caliber Hospitality Trust via forward purchase to recycle capital and reduce disposition risk.
  • Hyatt supporting Steamboat with ~$1.14M in brand capital; investment offerings available to accredited investors (feeder fund or project-specific).

Original SEC Filing: CaliberCos Inc. [ CWD ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

data.title: Teck quarterly profit beats estimates on record copper sales, higher prices
data.description:
Teck Resources on Thursday beat analysts' estimates for first-quarter profit, aided by an increase in copper prices and record sales, while the company's proposed merger with Anglo American remained on track.

The Canadian miner said it expects the Middle East conflict to push up freight and explosives costs through the second quarter, particularly at its Chilean operations which rely on imported diesel.

Teck and its peers are set to benefit from an expected 50% surge in global copper demand by 2040 on increasing power consumption by data centers to meet artificial intelligence and defense growth.

A sustained need for copper-intensive power, grid, and electronics infrastructure would support stronger long-term prices and volumes.

Average copper prices surged about 36.7% in the first quarter from a year earlier and hit all-time highs late in January on supply constraints, low inventory and strong demand.

Teck said its copper prices averaged $5.83 per pound in the quarter ended March 31, up from $4.24 a year earlier.

Copper production jumped 32% to 140,000 tons.

Production at the Quebrada Blanca mine in Chile increased 31.2% to 55,500 tons amid a ramp-up in operations.

Copper sales jumped 46% to 155,000 tons.

The miner reported adjusted earnings of C$1.75 per share for the quarter. Analysts on an average expected C$1.15 per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

Teck and Anglo shareholders voted in December for a $53 billion merger, paving the way for the creation of a copper heavyweight if regulators approve.

London-listed Anglo said last month that it expects to get final regulatory approval for the merger between September this year and March 2027.

data.title: Stocks to Watch: Tesla, IBM, Nestle, Lululemon — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

Tesla (TSLA): The EV maker reported better revenue and earnings in the first quarter-and surprised Wall Street with positive free cash-yet shares are down more than 2% premarket. Investors are drilling on the company's announcement that it plans to spend $25 billion on capital expenditures this year as it pursues its AI ambitions.

IBM (IBM): The tech company reported rising revenue and a higher profit in the first quarter. Still, shares are down more than 7% premarket after the company disappointed investors by keeping its revenue guidance the same.

Nestle (CH:NESN): The maker of KitKat chocolate bars and Nescafe coffee reported a drop in sales, but said real internal growth-which measures the volume of products sold-exceeded expectations. That's a metric the company's new CEO has prioritized in his turnaround efforts. Shares jumped 6%.

Lululemon (LULU): The athletic apparel company announced that it is picking longtime Nike executive Heidi O'Neill to be its next leader as it works to shore up its U.S. business. Shares fell nearly 4% premarket.

American Express (AXP), Lockheed Martin (LMT), American Airlines (AAL) and Intel (INTC) are all due to report earnings today.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: UK manufacturers report plunge in confidence, see jump in costs
data.description:
British manufacturers have turned their most pessimistic since the start of the COVID-19 pandemic due to the Iran war and a measure of the expectations for inflation has surged, according to a survey published on Thursday.

The Confederation of British Industry also showed firms' investment plans for buildings, plant and machinery and training were the weakest since April 2020.

"Warning signs are flashing in this survey," Ben Jones, the CBI's senior lead economist, said. "It's clear that the war in the Middle East is contributing to rising uncertainty, with supply chains beginning to see some renewed strain and cost pressures intensifying."

The survey's quarterly measure of optimism about the business outlook tumbled to -65 in April from -19 in January.

The CBI's monthly order book balance sank to -38 from -27 in March, falling further below its long-run average of -14.

The survey's gauge of expected prices jumped to +32 from +12 in March, representing the biggest month-to-month increase since records began in 1975.

The survey was based on responses from 276 manufacturers received between March 25 and April 13.

data.title: U.K. Grocer Sainsbury's Cautions on Spillover From Middle East War — Update
data.description:
By Aimee Look

British grocer Sainsbury's reported a rise in underlying pretax profit for the fiscal year ended in February, but warned that the war in the Middle East would weigh on its business.

Customers are increasingly fixated on the cost of living amid the conflict in the Middle East, Chief Executive Simon Roberts said. Sainsbury's said it is accounting for the duration and scale of the war in its profit guidance for the 2027 fiscal year.

The company is targeting total underlying operating profit of between 975 million pounds and 1.075 billion pounds for the fiscal year.

Rival grocer Tesco broadened its guidance for the year last week amid the uncertainty stemming from the Iran war. On Thursday, travel retailer WHSmith said it was suspending its dividend given uncertainty over passenger numbers ahead of the key summer season.

Sainsbury's underlying pretax profit, which strips out exceptional and other one-off items, was 718 million pounds ($969 million) for the fiscal year ended Feb. 28. The previous fiscal year, underlying pretax profit was 709 million pounds.

Meanwhile, retail underlying profit fell 1.1% year over year to 1.03 billion pounds, which the company attributed to operating cost inflation and investment in the company amid a competitive market. Shares slumped around 5% at 335 pence in early trading.

Group sales were 33.65 billion pounds, up from 32.77 billion pounds the year prior. Grocery sales increased 5.2%, while retail sales--excluding fuel--grew 4.3%.

Write to Aimee Look at aimee.look@wsj.com

data.title: Intel Stock Rises Again Ahead of Earnings. Thank Tesla and Elon Musk. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Levine

Intel stock has been on a tear, up 235% in the past year after a multiyear slide. As the company reports its first-quarter earnings Thursday afternoon, investors will be looking beyond this year to see if the company can find the elusive outside customers it needs for its manufacturing.

Wall Street analysts polled by FactSet expect first-quarter adjusted earnings-per-share of 2 cents, down from 13 cents last year. Sales are projected to decline by 2% from last year to $12.4 billion.

Intel stock has been on a long journey. The company had manufacturing technology problems for years, which led to a change in direction in 2021 under new CEO Pat Gelsinger. For the first time, the company would try to contract out its manufacturing like the leader in that business, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing. From the stock's 2020 peak at $69.29, it fell to $17.67 a year ago. As late as last August, the price was $18.97.

But a new deal-making CEO, Lip-Bu Tan, has shaken up the narrative. First, he sold a 9% share of the company to the U.S. government, earning the favor of President Donald Trump. Then he formed a loose partnership with Nvidia, the AI-chip leader, that included a 4.5% stake in Intel for Nvidia.

The narrative was boosted in the past month by Intel buying back a portion of a factory it had previously sold to Apollo Global Management, as well as a new deal with Elon Musk's companies to build a massive factory complex in Texas.

Intel stock reached a new high of $70.33 last week, despite a near-term outlook that is tepid. As a result, the stock is trading at 92 times its projected earnings-per-share for the next 12 months, a record for the company. The broad S&P 500 index trades at around a 21 multiple to earnings.

Intel has the same three big problems it had a year ago. First, its manufacturing technology still lags Taiwan Semi, and it has not yet convinced a large outside customer to use its production lines. Outside customers are key, because Tan has said that the next step in Intel's manufacturing technology will be too expensive to build without external income. The Intel foundry segment only has a single, internal customer, and analysts project it will show a $2.4 billion first-quarter operating loss.

The internal customer is primarily a seller of PC chips, which analysts expect to make up 57% of first-quarter Intel sales. The PC business is being badly impacted by a global memory shortage that is driving up prices, and analysts see first-quarter sales to this end market slipping by 7% from the year before.

Intel's final problem is that it has missed out on much of the revenue generated by trillions of dollars being invested into AI data centers. The primary beneficiary has been Nvidia. In a two-way data center revenue race between the companies, Intel held a 71% share in 2021, which dwindled to 7% last year.

So the narrative around Intel is not about this year or even next year, but whether it can bring in outside customers to turn its foundry operating loss into a profit. Support from the White House, Musk, and Nvidia could help.

Write to Adam Levine at adam.levine@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: UK private sector activity picks up in April, cost inflation spikes
data.description:
The S&P Global flash PMI composite output index rose to 52.0 this month from 50.3 in March. A reading above 50.0 indicates expansion, while a reading below signals contraction.

The services PMI business activity index printed at 52.0 in April, up from 50.5 the month before, while the PMI for manufacturing was 51.8 versus 49.2 in March.

Chris Williamson, chief business economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence, said the economy had gathered some renewed momentum in April after the initial impact of the Iran war caused growth to stall in March. However, the upturn came with a catch.

"The improved rate of expansion is in part a reflection of a short-term boost from a rush to secure purchases ahead of feared price rises and supply shortages linked to the war," he said.

"Prices have spiked higher at a rate not previously seen by the survey outside of the pandemic, suggesting inflation could rise more than many forecasters have been anticipating. Prices are rising not just because of surging energy costs, but also due to increases in charges levied for a wide variety of goods and services, with price hikes often stoked by supply concerns. The number of supply delays reported has jumped to the highest on record if the pandemic is excluded.

"Business confidence and employment have also been dragged lower by the ongoing conflict, boding ill for growth to weaken in the coming months just as price pressures intensify."

Williamson said the survey highlights the difficult choices faced by the Bank of England.

"The spike in price pressures will add to calls for rate hikes to dampen inflation, but the Bank will need to carefully assess the degree to which economic growth might weaken," he said. "While April’s PMI is indicative of the economy rebounding from a flat picture in March to a 0.2% quarterly growth rate, the details of the survey hint strongly that this pace cannot be sustained should the crisis persist."

data.title: Granblue Environment To Buy Asset, Trading To Resume
data.description:
Grandblue Environment Co Ltd :

  • SAYS TRADING TO RESUME FROM APRIL 24

  • SAYS IT PLANS TO BUY STAKES IN INVESTMENT FUND, CANVEST ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION WITH UP TO 1.2 BILLION YUAN ($175.61 million) RAISED VIA SHARE ISSUANCE, CASH

($1 = 6.8332 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: Aayan Leasing And Investment Signs New Credit Facility Agreement Valued At KD 35 Mln
data.description:
Aayan Leasing and Investment Company KSCP :

  • AAYAN LEASING AND INVESTMENT - CANCELS PREVIOUS CREDIT FACILITY AGREEMENT VALUED AT KD 25 MILLION WITH A LOCAL BANK

  • AAYAN LEASING AND INVESTMENT - SIGNS NEW CREDIT FACILITY AGREEMENT VALUED AT KD 35 MILLION WITH THE SAME BANK
